label: Google Maps Platform
description: Query Google Maps Platform APIs for geocoding, places search, routes, and distance matrix calculations.

form.title: Connect Google Maps Platform
form.subtitle: Paste your API key to enable Places, Geocoding, Routes, and Distance Matrix access.

methods.label: API Key
methods.description: Paste an API key from Google Cloud Console. The simplest path — no OAuth flow, no browser pop-up. Restrict the key to specific Maps APIs for security.
methods.how_to:
## Get a Google Maps Platform API Key

1. Open [Google Cloud Console](https://console.cloud.google.com/) and select or create a project.
2. Go to **APIs & Services → Library** and enable the APIs you need:
   - **Maps JavaScript API** (optional, for embed use)
   - **Places API**
   - **Geocoding API**
   - **Directions API** / **Routes API**
   - **Distance Matrix API**
3. Go to **APIs & Services → Credentials → Create credentials → API key**.
4. Click **Edit API key** on the newly created key.
5. Under **API restrictions**, select **Restrict key** and tick only the Maps APIs you enabled — this limits blast radius if the key leaks.
6. Optionally under **Application restrictions**, restrict to specific IP addresses or referrer URLs.
7. Copy the key (format: `AIza...`) and paste it below.

## Notes

- The key is shown at any time in Credentials — you don't need to capture it immediately.
- Google Maps Platform requires a billing account. Usage within the free monthly credit ($200) is common, but check [pricing](https://mapsplatform.google.com/pricing/) for your expected call volume.
- Rotate the key in Cloud Console if you suspect exposure; paste the new key here to update Anton.

fields.name: api_key
fields.label: API Key
fields.description: Your Google Maps Platform API key from Cloud Console → APIs & Services → Credentials.

fields.name: enabled_apis
fields.label: Enabled APIs
fields.description: Which Maps Platform APIs this key is authorized for. Used to validate calls and surface helpful errors.

fields.name: base_url_override
fields.label: Base URL override (optional)
fields.description: Leave blank to use the standard Google Maps endpoint. Set only if routing through a proxy or a Maps for Work enterprise endpoint.
